questions.question: What is the default network type that Docker assigns to a container if no network is specified?
What does the 'host' network type do?
options: Creates a completely isolated network for the container
Shares the host's network stack with the container, removing network isolation
Disables all networking for the container
Creates a private DNS server for the container
questions.answer: Shares the host's network stack with the container, removing network isolation

questions.question: What does the 'none' network type do?
options: Assigns a random network to the container
Connects the container to all available networks
Disables all networking for the container
Uses the host's network without isolation
questions.answer: Disables all networking for the container

questions.question: In the command 'docker run -p 8080:80 nginx', what does '8080' represent?
In the command 'docker run -p 8080:80 nginx', what does '80' represent?
What does the '-P' (capital P) flag do in 'docker run -P nginx'?
options: Maps all exposed ports to the same port numbers on the host
Maps all exposed ports to random available host ports
Opens all ports on the container without mapping
questions.answer: Maps all exposed ports to random available host ports

questions.question: Which command shows the port mappings for a running container?
Which command lists all Docker networks on the system?
Which command displays detailed information about a specific Docker network?
On the default bridge network, how can containers communicate with each other?
options: By both container name and IP address

questions.question: Why does DNS resolution (resolving container names) NOT work on the default bridge network?
options: DNS is disabled system-wide in Docker
The default bridge does not include Docker's built-in DNS service
Container names are not assigned on the default bridge
DNS only works with the 'host' network type
questions.answer: The default bridge does not include Docker's built-in DNS service

questions.question: Which command creates a user-defined (custom) bridge network called 'app-network'?
What key advantage does a custom bridge network have over the default bridge network?
options: Custom bridges are faster than the default bridge
Custom bridges provide automatic DNS resolution between containers by name
Custom bridges allow unlimited port mappings
Custom bridges automatically expose all ports to the host
questions.answer: Custom bridges provide automatic DNS resolution between containers by name

questions.question: How do you attach a container to a specific network at launch time?
If 'api' and 'web' containers are both on a custom bridge network called 'my-net', how can 'web' reach 'api' on port 5000?
Can a container on 'network-a' communicate with a container on 'network-b' by default?
options: Yes, all Docker containers can communicate regardless of network
Yes, but only if they share the same IP range
No, containers on different networks are isolated from each other
No, unless they both use the host network
questions.answer: No, containers on different networks are isolated from each other

questions.question: Which command retrieves the IP address of a container named 'server1'?
What does the '--name' flag do in 'docker run --name api my-image'?
options: Sets the hostname of the host machine
Assigns a name to the container that can also be used for DNS resolution on custom networks
Creates a DNS record in the system's /etc/hosts file
Tags the image with a new name
questions.answer: Assigns a name to the container that can also be used for DNS resolution on custom networks

questions.question: Which command removes a Docker network called 'app-network'?
What must you do before removing a Docker network that has running containers?
options: Run docker network disconnect for each container
Stop and remove the containers connected to the network
questions.answer: Stop and remove the containers connected to the network

questions.question: In the multi-container demo, why is 'host="0.0.0.0"' used in the Flask app's app.run() call?
options: It limits access to localhost only
It binds the server to all network interfaces, making it reachable from outside the container
It assigns a static IP address to the container
It disables the firewall inside the container
questions.answer: It binds the server to all network interfaces, making it reachable from outside the container

questions.question: What does the EXPOSE instruction do in a Dockerfile?
options: Automatically publishes the port to the host
Opens a firewall rule on the host machine
Documents which port the containerized application listens on
Creates a port mapping between host and container
questions.answer: Documents which port the containerized application listens on

questions.question: In the assignment, what is used to test communication between containers on a custom network?
options: docker exec to run curl from one container to another by name
Opening a browser on the host
questions.answer: docker exec to run curl from one container to another by name

questions.question: What flag builds a Docker image using a non-default Dockerfile name like 'Dockerfile.api'?
When running 'docker run --rm --network app-network python:3.12-slim ...', what does the '--rm' flag do?
options: Removes the network after the container stops
Removes the image after the container stops
Automatically removes the container when it exits
Runs the container in read-only mode
questions.answer: Automatically removes the container when it exits
